reflection: Before any action, ask: why am I doing this? The same deed can be worship or habit depending on what lives in your heart.

arabic: الْمُسْلِمُ مَنْ سَلِمَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ مِنْ لِسَانِهِ وَيَدِهِ
english: The Muslim is the one from whose tongue and hand other Muslims are safe.
reflection: Character is measured not in isolation but in community. How do people feel after they've been with you?

english: The strong man is not the one who can overpower others. Rather, the strong man is the one who controls himself when angry.
reflection: Self-mastery is the highest form of strength. Any man can react. The exceptional man responds with wisdom and restraint.

english: Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.
reflection: Not a recommendation. An obligation. The pursuit of beneficial knowledge is an act of worship built into the very foundation of the faith.

english: Take benefit of five before five: your youth before old age, your health before sickness, your wealth before poverty, your free time before preoccupation, and your life before death.
reflection: Time is the only resource you cannot earn back. Each of these five gifts is temporary. Act before the window closes.

english: None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.
reflection: True brotherhood requires active generosity of spirit — wishing success, ease, and guidance for others as sincerely as you wish it for yourself.

english: The best of you are those who learn the Quran and teach it.
reflection: The highest rank is given to those who both receive and transmit the Book of Allah. Learning is incomplete without sharing.

english: Whoever treads a path seeking knowledge, Allah will make easy for him a path to Paradise.
reflection: The path to Paradise is made smooth for the student. Every step you take in learning is a step toward a better destination.

english: A person's feet will not move on the Day of Resurrection until he is asked about four things: his life and how he spent it, his knowledge and what he did with it, his wealth and where he earned and spent it, and his body and how he used it.
reflection: You will be asked about your time, knowledge, wealth, and body. Are you building them or wasting them? This app exists to help you answer well.

english: Pray standing; if you cannot, then sitting; if you cannot, then on your side.
reflection: Prayer is not optional — only its form adapts to your circumstances. Make it non-negotiable no matter what.

english: The believer to another believer is like a building whose different parts support one another.
reflection: Isolation weakens the structure. Surround yourself with men who elevate, challenge, and carry you. Build the ummah, and it will carry you.

english: Make things easy, not difficult. Give good news and do not drive people away.
reflection: Ease is a mercy. When you lead, guide, or advise — lead with compassion. Rigidity repels; wisdom attracts.

english: Every act of service to a non-judicial organ of the body is an act of charity.
reflection: Taking care of your body, removing harm from the path, even a smile — all of it is charity. Islam dignifies every positive act.
